message:
test(ftui-extras): improve mermaid_diff.rs test coverage for edge cases and untested paths (bd-2b8nu)

Add 25 new tests covering edge label changes, node class changes,
duplicate edge matching, self-loop edges, DiffNode/DiffEdge index tracking,
node reordering stability, DiffStatus equality, DiffColors RGB constants,
DiagramDiff::is_empty edge cases, recolor_rect_border/dim_rect_interior
with zero/tiny rects, render_diff with changed edges and small areas,
total node count consistency invariant. Test count 20 → 45.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2b82","title":"Text Effects: Demo Showcase Integration","description":"## Background
The visual_effects screen needs to showcase all new text effects in an impressive, navigable demo that serves as both documentation and a selling point for FrankenTUI.

## Implementation

### Navigation Structure
```
Tab 1: Gradients    - Horizontal, Vertical, Diagonal, Radial, Animated
Tab 2: Animations   - Wave, Bounce, Shake, Cascade, Pulse, Breathing
Tab 3: Typography   - Shadow, Glow, Outline, Mirror, ASCII Art
Tab 4: Special FX   - Glitch, Chromatic, Scanline, Particle, Matrix
Tab 5: Presets      - All 10 presets with descriptions
Tab 6: Combinations - User can toggle effects on/off
```

### Key Features
1. Spacebar cycles through effects within current tab
2. TransitionOverlay shows effect name + description on switch
3. Number keys 1-6 switch tabs
4. 'e' key toggles easing comparison mode
5. 'c' key enters combination builder

### Visual Layout
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Gradients] [Animations] [Typography] ...    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                             │
│         EFFECT DEMO TEXT                    │
│         (with current effect)               │
│                                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Effect: Rainbow Gradient                    │
│ Speed: 0.3  │  Easing: EaseInOut           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Space: next │ ←→: adjust │ Tab: category   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### ASCII Art Header
Use AsciiArtText with Block style for 'EFFECTS' title, animated gradient.

## Files
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs

## Testing

### Unit Tests
- test_tab_navigation: Keys switch tabs correctly
- test_effect_cycling: Spacebar advances effect index
- test_transition_triggers: Effect change starts transition

### E2E Tests (in scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh)
```bash
# Navigate through all tabs and effects
echo 'Testing visual effects screen navigation...'
# Send key sequence: 1,space,space,2,space,3...
# Verify no panics, all effects render
```

## Acceptance
- All 6 tabs implemented
- All effects showcased
- Smooth transitions between effects
- No rendering glitches
- Help text updated with new keybindings

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Demo Showcase Integration
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-35t3s","title":"Diagram Type: Flowchart / Graph","description":"Background
- Flowchart is the most common Mermaid type and highest value.

Scope
- Parse flowchart syntax (TD/LR/TB/RL, node shapes, edge labels, click/link).
- Support subgraphs/clusters with titles and direction overrides.
- Use Sugiyama layered layout with crossing minimization.
- Orthogonal edge routing with arrowheads and labels.
- Support link styles, class styles, and per-edge line styles.

Considerations
- Handle cycles via deterministic edge reversal.
- Keep labels within bounds; use truncation when needed.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for parse + layout of canonical flowcharts.
- Snapshot tests at 80x24, 120x40, 200x60.
- PTY E2E logs: diagram_type=flowchart, size, tier, crossings, route_cost, warnings.

Success Criteria
- Flowcharts render cleanly at small sizes and scale to large screens.
- Crossings minimized; no overlaps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenCove","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:39:37.746268777Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:17:11.025746739Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:17:11.025721331Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35t3s","depends_on_id":"bd-12o4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:43:18.906272017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-35t3s","depends_on_id":"bd-17w24","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:43:26.312304087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-35t3s","depends_on_id":"bd-1ri6i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:43:08.594955568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-35t3s","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:43:13.272777286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-35t3s","depends_on_id":"bd-2hxl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:43:16.260590678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-35t3s","depends_on_id":"bd-2oovu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:03.105759466Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-35t3s","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:02.865943706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-35t3s","depends_on_id":"bd-33fdz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:51:09.722233115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-35t3s","depends_on_id":"bd-pt0dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:43:03.824625389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-35t3s","depends_on_id":"bd-u4fgt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:43:23.134687643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],  {"id":"bd-36hkk","title":"test(differential): re-enable SGR bg fixtures after VT styled_blank fix","description":"Fix VirtualTerminal reference implementation so that erase and insert operations (ECH, EL, ED, ICH, DCH, IL, DL, SU, SD) use the current SGR background color for blank cells instead of VCell::default(). This matches VT220 spec behavior and brings the reference in line with frankenterm-core.

Currently: blank cells from erase/insert ops get VCell::default() (no style)
Expected: blank cells inherit current_style.bg from the cursor

Impact: Once fixed, ~9 removed differential test fixtures can be re-enabled, and the differential test suite validates SGR attributes across all erase/insert operations.

Surface: crates/ftui-pty/src/virtual_terminal.rs (implementation methods only, not tests module)
Validation: cargo test -p ftui-pty --lib; cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test differential_terminal; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy","notes":"VirtualTerminal already fixed in cf2f07de (styled_blank uses current_style). Now re-adding the ~9 differential test fixtures that were removed during bd-1wadp.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:27:23.611306937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:30:28.379610596Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:30:22.434434139Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-36k2","title":"Gradients: Perceptual Sampling (OkLab) + DeltaE Monotonicity","description":"## Goal
Make gradients look visually uniform by interpolating in a perceptual color space (OkLab) and validating monotonic perceptual distance.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Convert RGB -> OkLab, interpolate linearly in OkLab, convert back to RGB.
This reduces banding and makes vertical/diagonal gradients feel smoother at low terminal color depth.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Interpolation in OkLab yields smoother perceived steps than linear RGB.
- DeltaE (perceptual distance) across steps is monotonic within tolerance.
- No additional per-character allocation on hot path.

## Implementation Notes
- Precompute conversion matrices and reuse.
- Clamp out-of-gamut values safely.
- Provide fallback to RGB interpolation when OkLab disabled.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_oklab_roundtrip_identity: convert -> back preserves color within epsilon.
- unit_deltae_monotonic: successive gradient samples have non-decreasing DeltaE.
- unit_out_of_gamut_clamp: no overflow or panic.
- snapshot_compare_rgb_vs_oklab: visual diff snapshots for the same gradient.

## Perf
- Microbench gradient sampling (p50/p95). Must stay within +5 percent vs RGB baseline.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-376","title":"Implement interactive widgets (List, Scrollbar, Input)","description":"PHASE 5 - INTERACTIVE WIDGETS

## Background
Interactive widgets handle user input and state changes. List provides selectable items, Scrollbar visualizes scroll position, Input accepts text entry.

## Technical Requirements

### List Widget
- Items with optional styling per item
- Single/multi selection modes
- Keyboard navigation (up/down/pageup/pagedown/home/end)
- Highlight style for selected item
- Scroll into view on selection change
- Item filtering/search support

### Scrollbar Widget
- Vertical and horizontal orientation
- Track, thumb, and arrow styles
- Thumb size proportional to content ratio
- Click/drag support (if mouse enabled)
- Auto-hide when content fits

### Input Widget
- Single-line text input
- Cursor positioning with blinking
- Text selection (shift+arrow)
- Clipboard operations (copy/paste)
- Placeholder text when empty
- Password mode (masked input)
- Input validation hooks

## Design Rationale
These widgets encapsulate common interaction patterns. State management follows Elm architecture - widgets emit events, parent handles state updates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List with selection modes
- [ ] List keyboard navigation
- [ ] Scrollbar sizing algorithm
- [ ] Scrollbar orientation support
- [ ] Input cursor management
- [ ] Input text selection
- [ ] All widgets implement StatefulWidget trait
- [ ] Event emission for state changes

## StatefulWidget Trait
pub trait StatefulWidget {
    type State;
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, state: &mut Self::State);
}

## Location: ftui-widgets crate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:19:33.823793890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:28:07.344066090Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:28:07.343967213Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-376","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-376","depends_on_id":"bd-wr2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
